dialogue.speaker: Elder Mira
dialogue.text: Rowan, you picked a loud day to arrive. Vael's midsummer wind festival only happens when the shrine bells still trust the sky.

dialogue.speaker: Elder Mira
dialogue.text: Lyra is preparing the shrine-song beside the dead Skywell. Dead, we call it. Old people know better.

dialogue.speaker: Elder Mira
dialogue.text: If the ground shakes, do not run west with the crowd. Find Bronn by the eastern road. He knows how Helion engines fail.

dialogue.speaker: Elder Mira
dialogue.text: Press M and open Story if you forget the thread: festival, Skywell disaster, forest road, cave shrine, Weather Guardian.

dialogue.speaker: Guard Toma
dialogue.text: Hold it. Eastern road is festival-closed until Lyra finishes the shrine-song. Official words. Bad feeling.

dialogue.speaker: Guard Toma
dialogue.text: I saw engine-wisps in Greenwood last night. Little blue things. Like lanterns with teeth.

dialogue.speaker: Guard Toma
dialogue.text: Bronn says the old Skywell is not a relic. He says it is a lock. I hate when grim men are specific.

dialogue.speaker: Guard Toma
dialogue.text: Move with WASD or arrows. Press B to test battle. Press M to read the Chapter 1 story log.

dialogue.speaker: Merchant Pip
dialogue.text: Fresh potions for festival travelers! Sky tonics for people who ignore ominous shrine noises!

dialogue.speaker: Merchant Pip
dialogue.text: Lyra's song is supposed to wake the festival lanterns, not the Skywell. Important distinction.

dialogue.speaker: Merchant Pip
dialogue.text: If Vael starts falling apart, Greenwood Road leads to the cave shrine. Nobody buys snacks after a cave shrine, so stock up now.

dialogue.speaker: Merchant Pip
dialogue.text: Use items from the menu or in battle. You will want them before the Broken Weather Guardian.
